% Simulates a sideways "scan": a portrait page whose entire content is
% rotated 90 degrees, with NO /Rotate attribute — the case manual view
% rotation (Phase 1) must fix. All glyphs report a 90-degree angle.
\documentclass[12pt]{article}
\usepackage[paperwidth=8.5in,paperheight=11in,margin=0.5in]{geometry}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\pagestyle{empty}
\setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
\begin{document}

\noindent
\rotatebox{90}{%
\begin{minipage}{9in}
SCANMARK

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This entire page body is
rotated ninety degrees with no Rotate attribute, imitating a sideways
scan. After manual view rotation the text should read normally and the
line detector should recover these sentences as horizontal bands.

Final line of the sideways body.
\end{minipage}}

\end{document}
